content.text: Risk mitigation plans authored before build starts ensure that engineers know exactly what guardrails, eval requirements, and operational controls to build into the system — not add later as afterthoughts. A mitigation plan converts each item in the risk register and safety impact assessment into concrete, verifiable engineering tasks. Without this step, risks are identified and scored but never actually reduced.

attrs.alt: Risk Mitigation Strategy Selection — 2×2 matrix: Mitigate, Transfer, Accept, Reduce Probability

content.text: Every risk in the register falls into one of four response strategies. The strategy is determined by the risk's residual probability and residual impact after theoretical mitigations are applied. Choosing the wrong strategy wastes engineering effort or leaves genuine danger unaddressed.
High probability AND high impact — the risk is both likely and damaging
Guardrails, confidence thresholds, scope restrictions, HITL escalation, adversarial eval suite
High impact, low probability — harm is severe but hard to prevent technically
Vendor liability clauses, cyber insurance covering AI-generated harm, legal indemnity language
Low impact, high probability — the risk fires often but each instance is recoverable
Input topic classifier to prevent off-scope queries, rate limiting, session context guards
Low probability AND low impact — mitigation cost exceeds expected harm
Vendor API price increase risk when total lock-in cost is low; minor formatting errors
Never accept a risk with a residual impact of CRITICAL or HIGH. These must be MITIGATED or TRANSFERRED before build starts, regardless of probability.

Each risk entry in the register gets a corresponding mitigation plan. The plan specifies the strategy, lists each concrete engineering task, assigns an owner, and sets an acceptance criterion that must pass before the feature ships.
Mitigation Plan for Each Standard Risk
The following plans correspond to the six standard risk entries (R1–R6) from the feature risk register. Each plan is fully specified before the first sprint begins.
Plan R1: Hallucination in High-Stakes Output

plan_r1 = MitigationPlan(
    plan_id="MP-R1",
    risk_id="R1",
    risk_title="Hallucination in high-stakes output",
    strategy=MitigationStrategy.MITIGATE,
    fallback_if_fails="Descope high-stakes domain from v1; ship general-purpose only",
)

plan_r1.add_task(MitigationTask(
    task_id="MP-R1-T1",
    description=(
        "Implement confidence threshold: if the model's self-reported confidence "        "is below 0.70, append a low-confidence disclaimer and suggest verification."
    ),
    owner="Engineering Lead",
    acceptance_criterion=(
        "Unit test: 100% of responses with confidence < 0.70 include the disclaimer string. "        "Eval: disclaimer does NOT appear on responses with confidence >= 0.70 (< 2% false positive)."
    ),
    linked_ticket="ENG-2401",
))

plan_r1.add_task(MitigationTask(
    task_id="MP-R1-T2",
    description=(
        "Eval gate: hallucination rate on the domain-specific eval dataset must be <= 5%. "        "Dataset: 200 factual Q&A pairs from authoritative sources, manually verified."
    ),
    owner="AI Safety Lead",
    acceptance_criterion=(
        "CI pipeline runs the eval dataset on every model version change. "        "Gate blocks deployment if hallucination_rate > 0.05."
    ),
    linked_ticket="ENG-2402",
))

plan_r1.add_task(MitigationTask(
    task_id="MP-R1-T3",
    description="Citation required: factual claims must include a source reference from the context window.",
    owner="Engineering Lead",
    acceptance_criterion=(
        "Eval: 95% of factual responses include a source citation. "        "Metric computed by the eval harness automatically."
    ),
    linked_ticket="ENG-2403",
))

print(plan_r1.is_build_ready)   # True — all tasks have owner and criterion
print(plan_r1.summary())
# {'plan_id': 'MP-R1', 'risk_id': 'R1', 'strategy': 'mitigate',
#  'total_tasks': 3, 'done': 0, 'blocked': 0,
#  'is_build_ready': True, 'is_launch_ready': False}

plan_r2 = MitigationPlan(
    plan_id="MP-R2",
    risk_id="R2",
    risk_title="Refusal policy miss — adversarial prompt bypasses guardrail",
    strategy=MitigationStrategy.MITIGATE,
    fallback_if_fails="Disable the feature until patch is deployed; SLA: 4h to patch",
)

plan_r2.add_task(MitigationTask(
    task_id="MP-R2-T1",
    description=(
        "Build adversarial eval suite: 60 jailbreak patterns covering prompt injection, "        "role-play bypasses, language switching, and encoding tricks (base64, Pig Latin)."
    ),
    owner="AI Safety Lead",
    acceptance_criterion=(
        "Suite executes in CI. All 60 cases must return a refusal or safe deflection. "        "0% bypass rate required before launch."
    ),
    linked_ticket="SAF-112",
))

plan_r2.add_task(MitigationTask(
    task_id="MP-R2-T2",
    description=(
        "Input guardrail: deploy topic classifier before LLM call. "        "Classifier blocks known out-of-scope categories with p >= 0.85 confidence."
    ),
    owner="Engineering Lead",
    acceptance_criterion=(
        "Classifier latency p99 < 50ms. Precision >= 0.90, recall >= 0.85 "        "on a 500-sample test set (balanced in-scope / out-of-scope)."
    ),
    linked_ticket="ENG-2404",
))

plan_r2.add_task(MitigationTask(
    task_id="MP-R2-T3",
    description=(
        "Output guardrail: content filter on response before rendering. "        "Filter checks for harmful categories (violence, self-harm, PII in output)."
    ),
    owner="Engineering Lead",
    acceptance_criterion=(
        "Filter catches 100% of synthetic harmful outputs in the test set. "        "False positive rate < 0.5% on benign outputs."
    ),
    linked_ticket="ENG-2405",
))
A single MitigationPlanRegistry holds all plans and exposes the pre-build and pre-launch gate checks used by the release process.
The mitigation plan review happens in the last sprint before development starts (the "sprint 0" or requirements freeze). The review has three outputs:
: every MitigationTask maps 1-to-1 to a ticket in Jira/Linear. The acceptance criterion becomes the Definition of Done.
returns True — no plan is missing an owner or criterion. Build does not start until this gate is green.
field is reviewed and approved by the Product Owner. If a mitigation cannot be completed (blocked dependency, third-party delay), the fallback activates automatically.
All T0/T1 risks have MITIGATE or TRANSFER strategy
No T0/T1 risk with strategy = ACCEPT
fallback_if_fails documented for every CRITICAL or HIGH risk plan

Guardrails designed post-hoc are retrofits — they add latency and fight the system prompt rather than complementing it. Eval criteria defined after the model is already integrated tend to be loose enough to pass the model as-is.
"The model should handle this correctly" is not an acceptance criterion. An acceptance criterion must be binary, executable, and reference a specific dataset, threshold, or review outcome.
ACCEPT strategy on a HIGH-impact risk.
Teams sometimes accept high-impact risks to avoid slowing the build. This creates a liability that is invisible in sprint tracking. The registry's
check must catch this at launch gate review.
Mapping one risk to one task.

Complex risks (hallucination, jailbreak) require layered mitigations. A single task per risk almost always underpowers the mitigation — the confidence threshold alone does not prevent hallucination without the eval gate and citation requirement.
Mitigation Plans for Remaining Standard Risks
R3 through R6 require mitigation plans before build starts. These tend to have simpler task lists than R1/R2, but the same ownership and acceptance-criterion discipline applies.

# R3: Token cost exceeds monthly budget
plan_r3 = MitigationPlan(
    plan_id="MP-R3",
    risk_id="R3",
    risk_title="Token cost exceeds monthly budget",
    strategy=MitigationStrategy.REDUCE_PROBABILITY,
    fallback_if_fails="Downgrade to cheaper model tier for non-critical requests",
)
plan_r3.add_task(MitigationTask(
    task_id="MP-R3-T1",
    description="Enforce 2,048-token hard limit on output per request at the API call layer.",
    owner="Engineering Lead",
    acceptance_criterion=(
        "Load test: 100% of synthetic p99 requests complete under 2,048 output tokens. "        "No user-visible truncation at the 2,048 boundary in the UX test."
    ),
    linked_ticket="ENG-2410",
))
plan_r3.add_task(MitigationTask(
    task_id="MP-R3-T2",
    description="Budget alert: automated alert at 70% of monthly token budget cap.",
    owner="Engineering Lead",
    acceptance_criterion=(
        "Alert fires in staging when a synthetic 70%-budget event is injected. "        "Alert reaches on-call channel within 60s."
    ),
    linked_ticket="ENG-2411",
))

# R4: PII in prompt
plan_r4 = MitigationPlan(
    plan_id="MP-R4",
    risk_id="R4",
    risk_title="PII included in prompt (data breach)",
    strategy=MitigationStrategy.MITIGATE,
    fallback_if_fails="Block the feature for users whose data flows through the affected pipeline",
)
plan_r4.add_task(MitigationTask(
    task_id="MP-R4-T1",
    description=(
        "PII detector in input guardrail: regex patterns for email, phone, SSN, DOB; "        "ML classifier for freeform PII (names, addresses) with threshold 0.80."
    ),
    owner="Engineering Lead",
    acceptance_criterion=(
        "PII recall >= 0.95 on a 200-sample test set (100 PII / 100 benign). "        "Precision >= 0.90 (avoid false positives that block valid queries)."
    ),
    linked_ticket="ENG-2412",
))
plan_r4.add_task(MitigationTask(
    task_id="MP-R4-T2",
    description="Zero-retention API mode enabled with the LLM provider for this feature's endpoint.",
    owner="Engineering Lead",
    acceptance_criterion=(
        "DPA (Data Processing Agreement) signed with provider and filed. "        "Zero-retention mode confirmed in provider dashboard before production rollout."
    ),
    linked_ticket="LEGAL-088",
))

# R5: Latency SLO miss
plan_r5 = MitigationPlan(
    plan_id="MP-R5",
    risk_id="R5",
    risk_title="Inference latency exceeds p95 SLO",
    strategy=MitigationStrategy.REDUCE_PROBABILITY,
    fallback_if_fails="Degrade gracefully: serve cached last-known-good response with stale notice",
)
plan_r5.add_task(MitigationTask(
    task_id="MP-R5-T1",
    description=(
        "Implement streaming output: first token delivered to UI within 300ms target. "        "Use server-sent events; UI shows typing indicator until stream completes."
    ),
    owner="Engineering Lead",
    acceptance_criterion=(
        "Load test at 2× expected peak: first-token latency p95 < 300ms. "        "Total response p95 < 2,500ms."
    ),
    linked_ticket="ENG-2413",
))

# R6: Vendor API deprecation
plan_r6 = MitigationPlan(
    plan_id="MP-R6",
    risk_id="R6",
    risk_title="Vendor API deprecation / lock-in",
    strategy=MitigationStrategy.ACCEPT,     # low impact + unlikely at build start
    fallback_if_fails="Activate secondary provider using abstraction layer within 48h",
)
plan_r6.add_task(MitigationTask(
    task_id="MP-R6-T1",
    description=(
        "Build LLM abstraction layer: single LLMClient interface over provider SDKs. "        "Both primary (Anthropic) and secondary (OpenAI) provider adapters implemented."
    ),
    owner="Engineering Lead",
    acceptance_criterion=(
        "Provider swap test passes: switching PROVIDER env var runs full integration test "        "suite with < 5% test delta between providers."
    ),
    linked_ticket="ENG-2414",
))

registry = MitigationPlanRegistry(feature_id="FEAT-2024-045")
for plan in [plan_r1, plan_r2, plan_r3, plan_r4, plan_r5, plan_r6]:
    registry.add(plan)

print(registry.is_build_gate_green())   # True
print(registry.status_report())
corresponds to one engineering ticket. The acceptance criterion becomes the ticket's Definition of Done. This creates a direct audit trail from a risk register entry to a closed sprint ticket.
100% of low-confidence responses include disclaimer; < 2% false positive rate
CI gate blocks deploy if hallucination_rate > 5% on 200-case dataset
95% of factual responses include source citation in eval harness
60 jailbreak cases in CI; 0% bypass rate before release
p99 latency < 50ms; precision >= 0.90; recall >= 0.85 on 500-case set
100% synthetic harmful outputs caught; < 0.5% false positive on benign set
DPA signed; zero-retention confirmed in provider dashboard

Tasks in Sprint 1 are build blockers — their acceptance criteria must pass before the first production-candidate build can proceed. Tasks in Sprint 2 and later must complete before the launch gate review.

Risk mitigation plans translate the risk register into specific engineering work items before a single line of feature code is written. Each plan picks a strategy (Mitigate, Transfer, Reduce Probability, or Accept), specifies tasks with owners and verifiable acceptance criteria, and defines a fallback if the mitigation cannot be completed. The MitigationPlanRegistry provides two gates: a build gate (all plans have owners and criteria) and a launch gate (all tasks are done).
: Mitigate = high impact + likely; Transfer = high impact + unlikely; Reduce Probability = low impact + likely; Accept = low impact + unlikely
a CRITICAL or HIGH impact risk — mitigate or transfer it before build starts
: description + owner + acceptance_criterion + linked_ticket — all required for build gate
— all plans have owners and criteria before sprint 1
— all tasks DONE before the feature ships
: approved by Product Owner at sprint-0 review; activate automatically if mitigation blocks
